exercises.title: PEAK OUTPUT
promptLines: Define **maxCell(cells)** returning the largest number in a non-empty array. Use **reduce**.
starterLines: // TODO: reduce, keeping the bigger of the two
solutionLines: return cells.reduce(function (m, c) { return c > m ? c : m; });
exercises.hint: cells.reduce((m, c) => (c > m ? c : m)) — with no seed, reduce starts from the first cell.
exercises.lore: One cell always runs hottest. Find it before it blows.

exercises.title: MEAN SIGNAL
promptLines: Define **average(nums)** returning the arithmetic mean of the array. An **empty** array averages to **0**.
starterLines: // TODO: guard the empty case, then sum / length
solutionLines: return nums.reduce(function (a, c) { return a + c; }, 0) / nums.length;

tests.name: empty -> 0
tests.code: assertEqual(average([]), 0, 'an empty stream averages to 0, not NaN');

exercises.hint: Return 0 when nums.length is 0; otherwise nums.reduce((a, c) => a + c, 0) / nums.length.
exercises.lore: Noise cancels. The mean is the only honest reading.

exercises.title: CONFIG OVERRIDE
promptLines: Define **mergeConfigs(a, b)** returning a **new** object with all keys of both, where `b`'s values win on conflicts. Do **not** mutate `a` or `b`.
mergeConfigs({ hp: 100, mp: 30 }, { mp: 50, lvl: 9 })  // { hp: 100, mp: 50, lvl: 9 }
starterLines: // TODO: copy a, then let b override

tests.name: b overrides a
tests.code: assertEqual(mergeConfigs({ hp: 100, mp: 30 }, { mp: 50, lvl: 9 }), { hp: 100, mp: 50, lvl: 9 });

tests.name: does not mutate a

exercises.hint: Object.assign({}, a, b) — later sources overwrite earlier ones, and the fresh {} keeps the inputs clean.
exercises.lore: Defaults are a suggestion. The override is the law.

exercises.title: BOUNTY LEDGER
exercises.brief: Tally every reward on the board.
promptLines: `board` is an object of `name -> reward`. Define **totalRewards(board)** returning the sum of all the rewards (the **values**). Use **Object.values** and **reduce**. An empty board totals **0**.
solutionLines: return Object.values(board).reduce(function (a, c) { return a + c; }, 0);
exercises.hint: Object.values(board).reduce((a, c) => a + c, 0) — pull the numbers out, then fold them.
exercises.lore: Add up every head on the board. That is your year.

exercises.title: REVERSE LOOKUP
exercises.brief: Flip the codec table end for end.
promptLines: Define **invert(obj)** returning a **new** object with keys and values swapped: each value becomes a key, mapping back to its original key. Assume values are unique. Use **Object.entries**.
starterLines: // TODO: for each [key, value], set out[value] = key
exercises.hint: Object.entries(obj).reduce((acc, [k, v]) => { acc[v] = k; return acc; }, {})
exercises.lore: Every codec runs both ways. Read it backward and the ghost speaks.
